signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.53; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% — 375bp carry differential favors USD. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (week Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. HV30 at 10.3%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 11.2% — options pricing a 0.9 vol-pt premium over realized. AUD/USD at 0.7145; 25d RR at −0.28: mild put skew. Late-trend caution signal — watch for LF delta turning negative to invalidate.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.349; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. ATM IV 9.5% near HV — options neutral.

signals.title: USD/CAD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoC at 2.25% (ON HOLD) vs Fed at 3.75% (ON HOLD) — 150bp CAD yield disadvantage. USD/CAD at 1.3668, testing the 1.3700 level. Positioning neutral (CFTC). A sustained break above 1.3700 could signal further USD strength, while a drop below 1.3600 might relieve some pressure on CAD.
